title: The Middle English Metrical Version of the Revelations of Methodius
summary: The General Corpus XML is a pinned repository copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00069. The encoded body is canonically identical to the current public U-M raw XML body, although the public file now adds a catalog header and rights statement. CME00069 selects only Charlotte D'Evelyn's internal section V: the 975-line Middle English poem on PMLA 33.2 (1918), pp. 156-182; it omits her study, post-text notes, and Latin appendix on pp. 135-155 and 182-203. D'Evelyn prepared the edition with Marguerite Thiebaud's transcript from manuscript rotographs and reports that the transcript was compared with the original Stowe MS 953. The poem is a version of abbreviated Latin Revelationes Recension 2, supplemented from Cursor Mundi and, more narrowly, Andrew of Wyntoun. Complete public scans of PMLA volume 33 and the 1895 Stowe catalog are cached with supporting sidecars and exact internal mappings. No public manuscript facsimile was found: the British Library's exact record exposes no Digitised Content, so the manifest records a paid whole-physical-object photography request for all sixteen manuscript folios plus binding and furniture surfaces, while mapping the poem separately to D'Evelyn's historical fols. 4r-19r and keeping f. 19v ownership material outside the target portion.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is the anonymous Middle English poem, not Pseudo-Methodius's source work, D'Evelyn's full article, the physical witness, or either XML wrapper.
The current BL record's sixteen-folio physical extent and D'Evelyn's fols. 4r-19v notation are preserved as different locator layers until current pencil foliation can be checked.
The 1895 catalog, BL rate card, and U.S. Copyright Office circular are supporting description, access, and rights sources; none is inserted into the textual derivation chain.
Marguerite Thiebaud's transcript is provisionally typed catalog_description because schema 1.0.0 has no historical editorial-intermediary type; it is not a second medieval witness.
No manuscript images were cached because no item-specific public image object was verified.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00069
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00069.
TEMPHEAD records a 2016-09-15 structural review that removed a prose fragment before the poem and a notes fragment after it, changed TRAILER to CLOSER, and adjusted notes and page breaks.
The pinned file is 67,461 bytes; it omits the full U-M HEADER present in the current public raw XML, but its canonical EEBO subtree exactly matches that current file.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00069
entities.description: A public manually keyed and proofread electronic text of D'Evelyn's internal section V, retaining the poem, editorial heading note, thirty textual notes, page and historical folio milestones, italic expansion typography, and final Amen while omitting the surrounding article study, notes section, and Latin appendix.
notes: The current public raw file is 72,765 bytes and has SHA-256 74435197b3603eeed7c6a1fadbd67d6d04a0cc6becbe0c4182cefcbfebb7f0d5; the pinned repository wrapper differs, but canonicalized EEBO bodies share SHA-256 40f552a54bdbb65e4c997d01d73b9b7ea0e479a074a850a24ace12e726ab697a.

entities.label: Charlotte D'Evelyn, The Middle-English Metrical Version of the Revelations of Methodius (1918)
entities.description: D'Evelyn's 69-page PMLA article: a study of Pseudo-Methodius and its English reception, discussion of the unique Middle English metrical version and its sources, the 975-line text, notes, and an appendix printing a short Latin text. CME00069 encodes only internal section V on pp. 156-182.

bibliographic.citation: Charlotte D'Evelyn, 'The Middle-English Metrical Version of the Revelations of Methodius; With a Study of the Influence of Methodius in Middle-English Writings,' PMLA 33, no. 2 (1918): 135-203, https://doi.org/10.2307/457111.
bibliographic.title: The Middle-English Metrical Version of the Revelations of Methodius; With a Study of the Influence of Methodius in Middle-English Writings
bibliographic.container_title: PMLA / Publications of the Modern Language Association of America

notes: Internal sections I-IV form the study through p. 156; V is the Middle English text; VI contains notes from p. 182; VII is the short Latin appendix through p. 203.

entities.label: British Library Stowe MS 953
entities.description: The unique known manuscript of the Middle English metrical Revelationes: a small paper item of sixteen medieval folios containing only the poem, with red ink indicating rhymes, sometimes incorrectly. D'Evelyn's historical foliation places the poem on fols. 4r-19r and an ownership inscription on f. 19v.

identifiers.scheme: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.notes: This is the witness date, not an exact date for translation.

holding.notes: Current exact BL record 040-001953801; all manuscripts and archives must be consulted at the Library in London.

physical_description.format: Small manuscript containing only this poem
notes: The current catalog gives total physical extent but no folio-by-folio current pencil sequence.
D'Evelyn and the 1895 catalog record front ownership/provenance material and the William Gilberd ownership inscription at the end; f. 19v is whole-object context outside the poem.
Red ink indicates rhymes, sometimes incorrectly; D'Evelyn states that no stanza divisions are marked in the manuscript.

entities.label: Marguerite Thiebaud's working transcript of Stowe MS 953
entities.description: The transcript of the Middle English text prepared by Marguerite Thiebaud from rotographs and used with her notes by D'Evelyn. D'Evelyn says the transcript was later compared with the original manuscript but does not name the comparer in that statement.
notes: Provisionally typed catalog_description because the schema lacks a historical working-transcript type; this is a material intermediary, not a medieval witness.
No present repository or public copy was identified in this review.

entities.label: Rotographs of Stowe MS 953 used by Marguerite Thiebaud
entities.description: The photographic reproductions from which Thiebaud prepared her transcript. Their maker, exact date, physical format, completeness, and present location are not named in D'Evelyn's article.
entities.reproduction_format: Rotographs; exact extent and carrier unknown
notes: The rotographs are not assumed to cover the binding, furniture, blanks, or even every manuscript surface; no whole-object facsimile claim is made.

entities.label: Abbreviated Latin Revelationes of Pseudo-Methodius, Recension 2
alternate_labels: Short Latin version of the Revelations of Methodius
entities.description: The abbreviated Latin Revelationes form normally transmitted in England and used as the principal source of the Middle English metrical translation. It descends from the Syriac apocalypse attributed pseudonymously to Methodius; the translator's exact physical Latin exemplar is unidentified.

date_statements.label: Latin translation and early recension history
date_statements.value: Latin translation about 732; abbreviated Recension 2 transmitted in England

notes: The historical attribution to Methodius of Patara is pseudonymous; it must not be transferred to authorship of the Middle English poem.

entities.label: Cursor Mundi
entities.description: The Middle English universal-history poem used by the Stowe translator as a supplementary source for creation, astronomy, traditions concerning Antichrist, Enoch and Elijah, and numerous verbal or rhyme correspondences. D'Evelyn treats the Latin Revelationes, not Cursor Mundi, as the principal source.

entities.label: Andrew of Wyntoun's Original Chronicle
entities.description: The Scottish verse chronicle that D'Evelyn identifies as a narrow supplementary source for the Stowe poem's account of Cain and Lamech's descendants and the pillars of clay and stone.

entities.label: Catalogue of the Stowe Manuscripts in the British Museum, volume 1 (1895)
entities.description: The historical printed catalog whose entry 953 on p. 637 describes the manuscript's contents, paper support, sixteen-folio extent, date, red rhyme marks, and provenance. It is supporting description, not a source of the Middle English text.

bibliographic.citation: British Museum, Department of Manuscripts, Catalogue of the Stowe Manuscripts in the British Museum, vol. 1, Text (London: Printed by order of the Trustees, 1895), 637.
bibliographic.title: Catalogue of the Stowe Manuscripts in the British Museum
bibliographic.publisher: Printed by order of the Trustees

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of PMLA volume 33 (1918)
entities.description: A complete 884-page Google-digitized volume scan uploaded to Internet Archive, with the D'Evelyn article at scan leaves n144-n212 and the encoded poem at n165-n191. The full volume, not a poem-only extract, is cached.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 884-page PDF with OCR and scan-data derivatives; source metadata reports Google digitization at 600 ppi

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of the 1895 Stowe catalog, volume 1
entities.description: A complete 850-page Internet Archive PDF reproduction of the 1895 catalog, digitized with Wellcome Library sponsorship. The Stowe 953 description is on printed p. 637, PDF p. 657, scan leaf n656; the full catalog is cached.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete 850-page PDF with OCR and scan-data derivatives; 350 ppi source metadata

entities.label: British Library digitisation and reproduction services rate card (2025/26 rates)
entities.description: The current public two-page rate card linked from the BL Digitisation Services page. It supports cost and format expectations for a mediated whole-object request and is not part of textual transmission.

entities.label: Library of Congress Name Authority File record for Charlotte D'Evelyn
entities.description: The current LCNAF authority record supplies D'Evelyn's exact life dates and persistent identifiers. It is biographical and rights evidence, not part of textual transmission.

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright (April 2026)
entities.description: Current federal duration guidance supporting the United States assessment of the 1918 edition. It is access-rights evidence, not textual lineage.

agents.name: Charlotte D'Evelyn
notes: The U-M header supplies 1889-. The current LCNAF record, revised 2025-08-04, supplies exact dates 23 May 1889-18 December 1977; its cited source for the newly added death date is a 2025 Find a Grave check, so the authority update is recorded transparently rather than treated as an archival vital record.

agents.name: Pseudo-Methodius
alternate_names: Anonymous author of the Apocalypse of Pseudo-Methodius
notes: Conventional pseudonym for the late-seventh-century source author; not Methodius of Patara and not the Middle English translator.

scope.notes: The wrappers differ because the current public raw XML adds a HEADER, stylesheet, and commented revision block. Canonicalized EEBO subtrees are identical, directly confirming content identity.

subject.description: CME00069 poem division with heading note and textual notes

object.description: Internal section V, Middle English text

scope.notes: The encoding excludes D'Evelyn's pp. 135-155 study, post-poem notes beginning on p. 182, and Latin appendix on pp. 192-203.

object.description: Thiebaud's working transcript of the English text

scope.notes: D'Evelyn says Thiebaud prepared a transcript of 'the English text here printed' and that she used the transcript and notes in continuing the study.

object.description: Rotograph extent used for the poem

scope.notes: The source establishes use of rotographs but does not prove complete physical-object coverage.

object.description: At least the manuscript text needed for Thiebaud's transcript

scope.notes: No claim is made that the rotographs include covers, endleaves, blanks, f. 19v, or all sixteen physical folios.

subject.description: Printed Middle English text and textual notes

scope.notes: D'Evelyn reports in the passive that the transcript 'has since been compared with the original MS.' The edition-level comparison is secure, but the individual comparer is not assigned.
This is a work-level translation relationship. It does not identify the translator's exact physical Latin exemplar.

locators: lines 825-878 and 881-904 (Antichrist traditions and verbal parallels)

object.description: Cursor Mundi passages identified by D'Evelyn

scope.notes: D'Evelyn calls Cursor Mundi supplementary: the Latin Revelationes still guides the narrative.

object.description: Original Chronicle passages compared by D'Evelyn

scope.notes: D'Evelyn calls the influence much slighter than Cursor Mundi but unmistakable; the relationship supplies her post-1424 terminus.

subject.description: D'Evelyn article inside the complete volume scan

scope.notes: The cached PDF and OCR remain complete to the annual volume; the relation scopes only the article reproduced within it.

access.access_method: Open the exact Archives and Manuscripts record
Consult in the British Library's London manuscripts reading room; registered readers with a recent pass may submit the online collection-item request form and quote Stowe MS 953
notes: The exact record says all manuscripts and archives must be consulted in London and gives Reader Pass timing conditions.

access.access_method: No item-specific public image link or complete digital object was found; use the mediated imaging order or ask BL Reference Services
access.format: No public complete manuscript facsimile verified
notes: The exact catalog's Digitised Content field is blank. This is a dated public-access finding, not proof that no internal photography, microfilm, or future digitization exists.
D'Evelyn's 1918 edition is a complete printed poem but not a manuscript facsimile; the old rotographs have unknown extent and whereabouts.

access.access_method: Submit the British Library Imaging Services beta order form, quote Stowe MS 953, request color photography of the complete physical object, and ask for a quote and reuse terms
access.contact: Digitisation Services; permissions@bl.uk under the January 2026 Imaging terms, while the service page separately directs reproduction enquiries to licensing@bl.uk
access.format: Digital reference or reproduction photographs; BL says original manuscripts cannot be scanned and may require expert photography
notes: Request the complete physical object: front and back covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, every front and rear flyleaf, blanks, all sixteen manuscript folios recto and verso, f. 19v ownership material, and edges where offered. Do not request only the poem.
Map the target poem separately as D'Evelyn fols. 4r-19r and ask BL to supply the corresponding current pencil foliation; keep f. 19v in the delivery but outside work_portion.

The public rate card lists photographic TIFF at £27.70 for the first image and £7.25 for subsequent single-page images, with bespoke complex handling at £58.19 per image. A whole manuscript with binding and furniture surfaces requires an item-specific quote and may fall outside standard rates.
Ordering or private research access does not establish redistribution, Git publication, or print-on-demand rights. Obtain written permission for any reproduction or publication.

rights.jurisdiction: Worldwide to the extent legally possible
rights.basis: The CME submodule LICENSE dedicates its software and source content to the public domain under the Unlicense; the underlying medieval text and 1918 U.S. edition are independently old, but image and foreign-jurisdiction questions remain separate.

rights.component: Current U-M raw XML, encoded Middle English text, and markup
rights.basis: The current item header says the work has been identified as public domain, free of known copyright restrictions including related and neighboring rights, and may be copied, modified, distributed, and performed even commercially. The collection page separately labels corpus materials No Copyright-United States.
notes: The United States scope follows the collection-level rights statement. The item's broader wording is preserved but not silently generalized into a worldwide legal conclusion.

rights.basis: The medieval poem predates modern copyright terms. This conclusion does not determine rights in photographs, catalog metadata, editorial notes, or provider-supplied reproductions.

rights.component: 1918 PMLA article, edited poem, notes, and appendix
rights.basis: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, revised April 2026, states that all works published in the United States before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain; this article was published in 1918.

rights.component: D'Evelyn's modern study, editorial notes, and arrangement outside the United States
rights.jurisdiction: Outside the United States, including the United Kingdom
rights.basis: The U.S. public-domain cutoff is jurisdiction-specific. LCNAF now gives D'Evelyn's death as 18 December 1977. Current UK Intellectual Property Office guidance states a usual term of seventy years after an author's death but warns that older works can be governed by exceptions; an ordinary life-plus-seventy calculation would run through 31 December 2047, yet the article's 1918 U.S. publication and historical foreign-work rules still require a jurisdiction-specific assessment. The Cambridge page also displays a 1918 MLA copyright notice.
notes: Do not restate the 2047 ordinary-term calculation as a final UK or worldwide expiry date without checking transitional and country-of-origin rules for this exact article.

rights.component: Internet Archive complete-volume scan and derivatives
rights.basis: Internet Archive item metadata supplies the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark. This provider assertion is recorded separately from U.S. statutory analysis and from Cambridge delivery terms.
notes: The Public Domain Mark is a rights label, not a license grant and not proof of public-domain status in every country.

rights.basis: The catalog was published in 1895, well before the current U.S. pre-1931 public-domain cutoff.

rights.component: Internet Archive Stowe catalog scan and derivatives
rights.basis: The Internet Archive metadata marks the scanned catalog with the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark.

rights.component: Any reference or reproduction photographs supplied through British Library Imaging Services
rights.jurisdiction: British Library contract and applicable copyright jurisdictions
rights.basis: The BL service page warns that supplied images may carry copyright, and the January 2026 Imaging Services terms say users must not copy or reproduce supplied material without written permission, potentially subject to fees. The underlying medieval text's public-domain status does not override those order terms.
notes: Private research receipt does not authorize public Git distribution, commercial publication, or print-on-demand reuse. Seek written permission and retain the order confirmation's exact terms.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript contractions, separated word parts, crossed double l, occasional dotted y, and uncertain or supplied readings
editorial_practices.representation: Contractions expanded in italics; yr supplied according to prevailing manuscript usage; separated parts joined by hyphens; supplied letters and words bracketed; manuscript readings and proposed corrections recorded in notes
editorial_practices.description: D'Evelyn's opening text note explicitly defines italic expansions and bracketed supplies. Italics in CME00069 therefore usually encode editorial expansion rather than emphasis and cannot be treated as a visual facsimile of Stowe MS 953.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Rhymes indicated, sometimes wrongly, in red ink; no manuscript stanza divisions
editorial_practices.representation: Black-print verse arranged into editorial stanza blocks, normally eight lines, with folio and line milestones; red rhyme marking is described but not reproduced as red
editorial_practices.description: The 123 printed and XML line groups are editorial organization, not physical manuscript stanza boundaries. The complete manuscript images are needed to study the original rhyme marks and layout.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Thiebaud's rotograph-derived transcript and Stowe MS 953 original
editorial_practices.representation: Printed text and textual notes prepared with the transcript and a later comparison against the original manuscript
editorial_practices.description: The material intermediary is retained explicitly. The passive statement that the transcript was compared with the original does not justify naming an individual collator.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English text and notes, pp. 156-191

editorial_practices.representation: Only internal section V on pp. 156-182, with its heading note and thirty textual notes, is encoded
editorial_practices.description: The electronic text is a complete encoding of the printed poem selection, not a complete encoding of the article or PMLA volume. The 2016 revision removed stray prose and notes fragments outside this intended boundary.

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME00069 source XML

evidence.citation: Current public raw XML for University of Michigan CME00069
evidence.summary: Confirms item title and agents, U-M publication statement, 2018 header production, source article, 27-page encoded extent, Middle English language, item-level public-domain/no-known-restrictions wording, and a complete EEBO body canonically identical to the pinned repository artifact.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page
evidence.summary: Describes the corpus as manually keyed from public-domain editions with in-house proofreading and basic TEI markup, offers raw XML, and labels the collection materials public domain in the United States under No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: Charlotte D'Evelyn, 'The Middle-English Metrical Version of the Revelations of Methodius,' PMLA 33.2 (1918), 135-203, complete-volume scan
evidence.summary: Direct scan and page-image inspection verifies article identity and boundaries, Thiebaud's transcript from rotographs, comparison with the original manuscript, the unique Stowe witness and historical foliation, proposed Norfolk dialect, source relations, post-1424 argument, verse structure, manuscript-versus-edition typography, and exact encoded section.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Core, PMLA article record for D'Evelyn 1918
evidence.locator: Information, extract, references, and copyright display
evidence.summary: Confirms author, full title, PMLA volume 33 issue 2, 1918 publication, pp. 135-203, DOI 10.2307/457111, article type, opening extract, and the current publisher-page copyright display.

evidence.citation: Crossref DOI record 10.2307/457111
evidence.summary: Confirms Charlotte D'Evelyn, full article title, PMLA container, volume 33 issue 2, pages 135-203, print year 1918, publisher MLA, and ISSNs 0030-8129 and 1938-1530.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Stowe MS 953
evidence.summary: Confirms exact shelfmark and record ID, incipit, poem contents, rough octosyllabic quatrains, red rhyme markings, paper support, sixteen-folio extent, fifteenth-century date, English language, provenance, physical-request route, and a blank Digitised Content field.

evidence.citation: Catalogue of the Stowe Manuscripts in the British Museum, volume 1 (1895), entry 953
evidence.locator: printed p. 637, PDF p. 657, scan leaf n656
evidence.summary: Direct page-image inspection verifies the historical description from which the current BL record substantially descends: contents, incipit, red rhyme marks, paper, sixteen folios, fifteenth-century date, and provenance.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scan-data for PublicationsOfTheModernLanguage33
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete 1918 volume identity, Google digitization, ARLIMA upload, 2016 public date, 884-leaf access object, 600-ppi scan metadata, direct PDF and derivative files, and Public Domain Mark URL.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata and scan-data for b29002618_0001
evidence.summary: Confirms catalog title, British Museum authorship, 1895 publication, volume 1, Wellcome sponsorship, May 2017 digitization, 350-ppi scan metadata, complete PDF and derivatives, and provider Public Domain Mark.

evidence.citation: Michael W. Twomey, 'The Revelationes of Pseudo-Methodius and Scriptural Study at Salisbury in the Eleventh Century,' in Source of Wisdom (University of Toronto Press, 2007), 370-386
evidence.summary: Modern reception study dates the Syriac source about 690, describes Greek and Latin translations, identifies the abbreviated Recension 2 normally transmitted in England, and situates three Middle English vernacular versions. It supports work-level source identity without identifying a physical exemplar.

evidence.citation: Middle English Dictionary quotation record for Methodius(3), Stowe 953
evidence.summary: Confirms the MED short title, shelfmark abbreviation, a1500 citation date, and incipit.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services
evidence.locator: Online ordering, required information, formats, delivery, and permissions
evidence.summary: Confirms that individuals and researchers may order collection images through the beta form, must supply shelfmark/title/folio details, may request reference or reproduction images, cannot receive scans from original manuscripts, should request color explicitly, and must assess permissions separately.

evidence.citation: British Library Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026
evidence.locator: Preservation, turnaround, pricing, and copyright/reproductions clauses
evidence.summary: Confirms single-page delivery, possible alternative format and pricing for difficult material, standard thirty-working-day processing after payment, and the requirement not to copy or reproduce supplied material without written permission, potentially with additional fees.

evidence.citation: British Library digitisation and reproduction services price list, 2025/26 rates
evidence.summary: Lists £15.25 first and £0.58 subsequent JPEG scan images, £27.70 first and £7.25 subsequent photographic TIFF images, and £58.19 bespoke complex-handling photography; states that formats, resolution, and alternatives may add cost.

evidence.citation: Library of Congress Name Authority File, D'Evelyn, Charlotte, 1889-1977, n 87908139
evidence.locator: authoritative label, birthDate, deathDate, source note, and 2025-08-04 revision metadata
evidence.summary: The current authority record gives life dates 23 May 1889-18 December 1977 and identifies the death-date addition as an August 2025 revision based on a cited Find a Grave check. It provides current national-authority evidence for the death year while leaving legal term calculations jurisdiction-specific.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of copyright (term)
evidence.locator: Rules by type of work or performance; exceptions to the general rules
evidence.summary: States the usual term for literary works as seventy years following the author's death and cautions that older works can be subject to varying exceptions. It supports a provisional ordinary-term calculation, not a final status determination for a 1918 U.S.-published article.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026

evidence.summary: States that all works published in the United States before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain; also explains separate duration rules for unpublished and later works.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME submodule LICENSE
evidence.summary: Dedicates the submodule software and source to the public domain and permits copying, modification, publication, commercial and noncommercial use under the Unlicense terms.

evidence.citation: Dated item-level search for a public complete facsimile of British Library Stowe MS 953
evidence.summary: On 2026-07-12 the exact BL record had no Digitised Content or thumbnail link, exact-shelfmark searches found no BL Digital Manuscripts or Images Online object, and no resolvable item-specific IIIF or whole-object download was verified. The result is a dated negative finding, not proof that internal or future images do not exist.

evidence.citation: CME00069 local source-cache audit
evidence.locator: twelve cached files; complete PMLA PDF and sidecars; complete Stowe catalog PDF and sidecars; current U-M raw XML; LCNAF JSON-LD; BL rate card; USCO Circular 15A
evidence.summary: Verified all recorded byte counts and SHA-256 values, 884-page and 850-page PDF integrity and page mappings, current-raw versus pinned EEBO canonical identity, and true hard-link identity between worktree and canonical cache. The existing BL rate card and Copyright Office circular were reused through hard links rather than redownloaded. No manuscript-image file is present.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply a complete reusable color facsimile of Stowe MS 953, and how does D'Evelyn's fols. 4r-19v notation crosswalk to current pencil foliation?
next_steps: Submit the Imaging Services order for the complete physical object: both covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all flyleaves and blanks, all sixteen manuscript folios recto and verso, f. 19v ownership material, and edges where offered.
Ask BL to identify every image with current pencil foliation and to map the poem corresponding to D'Evelyn fols. 4r-19r separately.
Obtain written terms for private retention, Git redistribution, scholarly or commercial publication, and print-on-demand use before exposing any supplied files.

open_questions.question: Do Marguerite Thiebaud's transcript, research notes, or Stowe MS 953 rotographs survive, and who compared the transcript with the original?
next_steps: Search Bryn Mawr institutional archives, D'Evelyn papers and correspondence, PMLA editorial records, and historical British Museum reproduction orders.

If located, record each object's repository, carrier, exact extent, date, maker, manuscript coverage, and relationship to the printed text without treating a partial rotograph set as a complete facsimile.

open_questions.question: Can modern scholarship refine the exact Latin Recension 2 exemplar, Cursor Mundi and Wyntoun dependencies, post-1424 date, or probable Norfolk localization?
next_steps: Collate complete manuscript images and text against current critical editions of Latin Revelationes R2, Cursor Mundi, and Wyntoun.
Test D'Evelyn's four dialect features against current LALME localization while keeping Norfolk provenance and linguistic origin separate.

open_questions.question: What item-level production record explains U-M's selection of only internal section V and the 2016 removal of surrounding prose and notes fragments?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for keying, selection, and revision records; retain the directly verified print-to-XML mapping and canonical body comparison meanwhile.

open_questions.question: What is the exact copyright status of D'Evelyn's 1918 study and editorial contributions in each intended publication jurisdiction outside the United States?
next_steps: Use the LCNAF-recorded death date of 18 December 1977, then assess the intended jurisdiction's transitional, country-of-origin, and rule-of-the-shorter-term provisions for a work first published in the United States in 1918; do not infer worldwide status from the U.S. pre-1931 rule, an ordinary life-plus-seventy calculation, or Internet Archive's Public Domain Mark.
